questions.question_text: Find the simple interest on ₹ 68000 at $16 rac{2}{3}$% per annum for 9 months.
questions.solution:
P = ₹ 68000, R = $ rac{50}{3}$% p.a. and T = $ rac{9}{12}$ years = $ rac{3}{4}$ years.
$	herefore$ S.I. = $ rac{P	imes R	imes T}{100}$ = ₹ $ inom{68000	imes rac{50}{3}	imes rac{3}{4}	imes rac{1}{100}}$ = ₹ 8500.

questions.question_text: Find the simple interest on ₹ 3000 at $6 rac{1}{4}$% per annum for the period from 4th Feb., 2009 to 18th April, 2009.
questions.solution:
Time = (24 + 31 + 18) days = 73 days = $ rac{73}{365}$ year = $ rac{1}{5}$ year.
P = ₹ 3000 and R = $6 rac{1}{4}$% p.a. = $ rac{25}{4}$% p.a.
$	herefore$ S.I. = ₹ $ inom{3000	imes rac{25}{4}	imes rac{1}{5}	imes rac{1}{100}}$ = ₹ 37.50.

questions.question_text: A sum at simple interest at $13 rac{1}{2}$% per annum amounts to ₹ 2502.50 after 4 years. Find the sum.
questions.solution:
Let sum be ₹ x. Then, S.I. = ₹ $ inom{x	imes rac{27}{2}	imes4	imes rac{1}{100}}$ = ₹ $ rac{27x}{50}$.
$	herefore$ Amount = ₹ $ inom{x+ rac{27x}{50}}$ = ₹ $ rac{77x}{50}$.
$	herefore$ $ rac{77x}{50}$ = 2502.50 $\Leftrightarrow$ x = $ rac{2502.50	imes50}{77}$ = 1625.
Hence, sum = ₹ 1625.

questions.question_text: The simple interest accrued on an amount of ₹ 2500 at the end of 6 years is ₹ 1875. What would be the simple interest accrued on an amount of ₹ 6875 at the same rate and for the same period?
A sum of ₹ 800 amounts to ₹ 920 in 3 years at simple interest. If the interest rate is increased by 3%, it would amount to how much?
questions.solution:
S.I. = ₹ (920 – 800) = ₹ 120; P = ₹ 800, T = 3 years
$	herefore$ R = $ inom{ rac{100	imes120}{800	imes3}}$% = 5%.
New rate = (5 + 3)% = 8%.
New S.I. = ₹ $ inom{ rac{800	imes8	imes3}{100}}$ = ₹ 192.
$	herefore$ New amount = ₹ (800 + 192) = ₹ 992.

questions.question_text: Adam borrowed some money at the rate of 6% p.a. for the first two years, at the rate of 9% p.a. for the next three years, and at the rate of 14% p.a. for the period beyond five years. If he pays a total interest of ₹ 11400 at the end of nine years, how much money did he borrow?
questions.solution:
Let the sum borrowed be x. Then,
$ inom{ rac{x	imes6	imes2}{100} +  rac{x	imes9	imes3}{100} +  rac{x	imes14	imes4}{100}}$ = 11400
$ inom{ rac{3x}{25} +  rac{27x}{100} +  rac{14x}{25}}$ = 11400 $\Leftrightarrow$ $ rac{95x}{100}$ = 11400 $\Leftrightarrow$ x = $ inom{ rac{11400	imes100}{95}}$ = 12000.
Hence, sum borrowed = ₹ 12,000.

questions.question_text: A certain sum of money amounts to ₹ 1008 in 2 years and to ₹ 1164 in $3 rac{1}{2}$ years. Find the sum and the rate of interest.
questions.solution:
S.I. for $1 rac{1}{2}$ years = ₹ (1164 – 1008) = ₹ 156.
S.I. for 2 years = ₹ $ inom{156	imes rac{2}{3}	imes2}$ = ₹ 208.
$	herefore$ Principal = ₹ (1008 – 208) = ₹ 800.
Now, P = ₹ 800, T = 2 years and S.I. = ₹ 208
$	herefore$ Rate = $ inom{ rac{100	imes208}{800	imes2}}$% = 13%.

questions.question_text: At what rate of simple interest a certain sum will be doubled in 15 years?
questions.solution:
Let principal = P. Then, S.I. = P and T = 15 yrs.
$	herefore$ Rate = $ inom{ rac{100	imes P}{P	imes15}}$% = $6 rac{2}{3}$%.

questions.question_text: On a certain sum, the simple interest at the end of $12 rac{1}{2}$ years becomes $ rac{3}{4}$ of the sum. What is the rate of interest p.c.p.a?
questions.solution:
Let principal = P. Then, S.I. = $ rac{3}{4}$P and T = $12 rac{1}{2}$ yrs.
$	herefore$ Rate = $ inom{ rac{100	imes rac{3}{4}P}{P	imes rac{25}{2}}}$% = $ inom{ rac{100	imes3	imes2}{4	imes25}}$% = 6%.

questions.question_text: In how many years will a sum of money double itself at $6 rac{1}{4}$% simple interest per annum?
questions.solution:
Let principal = P. Then, S.I. = P and R = $6 rac{1}{4}$%.
$	herefore$ Time = $ inom{ rac{100	imes P}{P	imes rac{25}{4}}}$ yrs = $ inom{ rac{100	imes4}{25}}$ yrs = 16 yrs.

questions.question_text: A certain sum of money becomes three times of itself in 20 years at simple interest. In how many years does it become double of itself at the same rate of simple interest?
questions.solution:
Let principal = P. Then, S.I. = 2P and T = 20 yrs.
$	herefore$ Rate = $ inom{ rac{100	imes2P}{P	imes20}}$% = 10%.
Now, principal = P, S.I. = P, R = 10%.
$	herefore$ Time = $ inom{ rac{100	imes P}{P	imes10}}$ yrs = 10 yrs.

questions.question_text: The simple interest on a sum of money is $ rac{4}{9}$ of the principal. Find the rate percent and time, if both are numerically equal.
questions.solution:
Let sum = ₹ x. Then, S.I. = ₹ $ rac{4x}{9}$.
Let rate = R% and time = R years.
Then, $ inom{ rac{x	imes R	imes R}{100}}$ = $ rac{4x}{9}$ or $R^2$ = $ rac{400}{9}$ or R = $ rac{20}{3}$ = $6 rac{2}{3}$.
$	herefore$ Rate = $6 rac{2}{3}$% and Time = $6 rac{2}{3}$ yrs = 6 yrs 8 months.

questions.question_text: The simple interest on a certain sum of money for $2 rac{1}{2}$ years at 12% per annum is ₹ 40 less than the simple interest on the same sum for $3 rac{1}{2}$ years at 10% per annum. Find the sum.
questions.solution:
Let the sum be ₹ x. Then, $ inom{ rac{x	imes12	imes5}{100	imes2}} -  inom{ rac{x	imes10	imes7}{100	imes2}}$ = 40
$ inom{ rac{3x}{10} -  rac{7x}{20}}$ = 40 $\Leftrightarrow$ x = (40 $\times$ 20) = 800.
Hence, the sum is ₹ 800.

questions.question_text: A sum was put at simple interest at a certain rate for 3 years. Had it been put at 2% higher rate, it would have fetched ₹ 360 more. Find the sum.
questions.solution:
Let sum = P and original rate = R. Then, $ inom{ rac{P	imes(R+2)	imes3}{100}} -  inom{ rac{P	imes R	imes3}{100}}$ = 360
$\Leftrightarrow$ 3PR + 6P – 3PR = 36000 $\Leftrightarrow$ 6P = 36000 $\Leftrightarrow$ P = 6000.
Hence, sum = ₹ 6000.

questions.question_text: What annual instalment will discharge a debt of ₹ 1092 due in 3 years at 12% simple interest?
questions.solution:
Let each instalment be ₹ x. Then, $ inom{x +  rac{x	imes12	imes1}{100}} +  inom{x +  rac{x	imes12	imes2}{100}} + x$ = 1092
$\Leftrightarrow$ $ rac{28x}{25} +  rac{31x}{25} + x$ = 1092 $\Leftrightarrow$ (28x + 31x + 25x) = (1092 $\times$ 25)
$\Leftrightarrow$ x = $ inom{ rac{1092	imes25}{84}}$ = 325.
$	herefore$ Each instalment = ₹ 325.

questions.question_text: A sum of ₹ 1550 is lent out into two parts, one at 8% and another one at 6%. If the total annual income is ₹ 106, find the money lent at each rate.
questions.solution:
Let the sum lent at 8% be ₹ x and that at 6% be ₹ (1550 – x).
$	herefore$ $ inom{ rac{x	imes8	imes1}{100} +  rac{(1550-x)	imes6	imes1}{100}}$ = 106
$\Leftrightarrow$ 8x + 9300 – 6x = 10600 $\Leftrightarrow$ 2x = 1300 $\Leftrightarrow$ x = 650.
$	herefore$ Money lent at 8% = ₹ 650. Money lent at 6% = ₹ (1550 – 650) = ₹ 900.

questions.question_text: Ashish borrowed a sum of money from a nationalised bank at 12% simple interest per annum and the same amount at 10% simple interest per annum both for the same period. He cleared the first loan 6 months before the scheduled date of repayment and repaid the second loan just at the end of the scheduled period. If in each case he had to pay ₹ 3250 as amount then how much and for what time did he borrow?
questions.solution:
Let each sum = ₹ x.
Let first sum be invested for T years and the second sum for $ inom{T- rac{1}{2}}$ years.
Then, x + $ inom{ rac{x	imes12	imes(T- rac{1}{2})}{100}}$ = 3250
$Arr$ 100x + 12xT – 6x = 3250000 ... (i)
$Arr$ 94x + 12xT = 32500
And, x + $ inom{ rac{x	imes10	imes T}{100}}$ = 3250 $\rArr$ 100x + 10xT = 325000 ... (ii)
From (i) and (ii), we get: 94x + 12xT = 100x + 10xT $\rArr$ 6x = 2xT
$Arr$ 2T = 6 $\rArr$ T = 3.
Putting T = 3 in (i), we get: 94x + 36x = 325000 $\rArr$ 130x = 325000 $\rArr$ x = 2500.

questions.question_text: A person lends out ₹ 9000 on the condition that the loan is payable in 10 months by 10 monthly equal instalments of ₹ 1000 each. Find the rate of simple interest charged.
questions.solution:
We have:
₹ 9000 + S.I. on ₹ 9000 for 10 months
= ₹ 10000 + S.I. on ₹ 1000 for (1 + 2 +...+ 9) months.
$Arr$ ₹ 9000 + S.I. on ₹ 1000 for 90 months
= ₹ 10000 + S.I. on ₹ 1000 for 45 months
$Arr$ S.I. on ₹ 1000 for 45 months = ₹ 1000.
$	herefore$ Rate = $ inom{ rac{100	imes1000	imes12}{1000	imes45}}$% = $ rac{80}{3}$% = $26 rac{2}{3}$%.

questions.question_text: A man invested $\frac{1}{3}$ of his capital at 7%; $\frac{1}{4}$ at 8% and the remainder at 10%. If his annual income is ₹ 561, find the capital.
questions.solution:
Let the total capital be ₹ x.
Then, $\left(\frac{x}{3}\times\frac{7}{100}\times1\right)+\left(\frac{x}{4}\times\frac{8}{100}\times1\right)+\left(\left\{1-\left(\frac{1}{3}+\frac{1}{4}\right)\right\}\times x\times\frac{10}{100}\times1\right)=561$
$\Rightarrow \frac{7x}{300}+\frac{x}{50}+\frac{x}{24}=561 \Rightarrow 51x = 561 \times 600 \Rightarrow x = \left(\frac{561\times600}{51}\right)=6600.$
Hence, capital = ₹ 6600.

questions.question_text: What would be the simple interest obtained on an amount of ₹ 5760 at the rate of 6 p.c.p.a. after 3 years.?
questions.solution:
$P = ₹ 5760, R = 6\%, T = 3 \text{ yrs.}$
$\therefore S.I. = \left(\frac{5760 \times 6 \times 3}{100}\right) = ₹ 1036.80.$

questions.question_text: A farmer borrowed ₹ 3600 at 15% simple interest per annum. At the end of 4 years, he cleared this account by paying ₹ 4000 and a cow. The cost of the cow is
questions.solution:
$P = ₹ 3600, R = 15\%, T = 4 \text{ yrs.}$
$\therefore S.I. = ₹ \left(\frac{3600 \times 15 \times 4}{100}\right) = ₹ 2160.$
$\text{Hence, amount after 4 years} = ₹ (3600 + 2160) = ₹ 5760.$
$\therefore \text{Cost of the cow} = ₹ (5760 - 4000) = ₹ 1760.$

questions.question_text: Ram borrows ₹ 520 from Gaurav at a simple interest of 13% per annum. What amount of money should Ram pay to Gaurav after 6 months to be absolved of the debt?
questions.solution:
$P = ₹ 520, R = 13\%, T = \frac{1}{2} \text{ yr.}$
$\therefore S.I. = ₹ \left(\frac{520 \times 13}{100} \times \frac{1}{2}\right) = ₹ 33.80.$
$\text{Hence, amount after 6 months} = ₹ (520 + 33.80) = ₹ 553.80.$

questions.question_text: At the rate of $8\frac{1}{2}\%$ p.a. simple interest, a sum of ₹ 4800 will earn how much interest in 2 years 3 months?
questions.solution:
$P = ₹ 4800, R = 8\frac{1}{2}\% = \frac{17}{2}\%,$
$T = 2 \text{ yrs } 3 \text{ mths} = 2\frac{1}{4} \text{ yrs} = \frac{9}{4} \text{ yrs.}$
$\therefore S.I. = ₹ \left(4800 \times \frac{17}{2} \times \frac{9}{4} \times \frac{1}{100}\right) = ₹ 918.$

questions.question_text: What will be the simple interest earned on an amount of ₹ 16,800 in 9 months at the rate of $6\frac{1}{4}\%$ p.a. ?
questions.solution:
$P = ₹ 16800, R = 6\frac{1}{4}\% = \frac{25}{4}\%, T = 9 \text{ mths} = \frac{3}{4} \text{ yr.}$
$\therefore S.I. = ₹ \left(16800 \times \frac{25}{4} \times \frac{3}{4} \times \frac{1}{100}\right) = ₹ 787.50.$

questions.question_text: The simple interest on ₹ 1820 from March 9, 2012 to May 21, 2012 at $7\frac{1}{2}\%$ rate will be
questions.solution:
$\text{Time} = (22 + 30 + 21) \text{ days} = 73 \text{ days} = \frac{1}{5} \text{ year.}$
$\therefore S.I. = ₹ \left(1820 \times \frac{15}{2} \times \frac{1}{5} \times \frac{1}{100}\right) = ₹ 27.30.$

questions.question_text: A shopkeeper with an overdraft facility at 18 percent with a bank borrowed ₹ 15000 on Jan 8, 2011 and returned the money on June 3, 2011 so as to clear the debt. The amount that he paid was
questions.solution:
$\text{Time} = (23 + 28 + 31 + 30 + 31 + 3) \text{ days} = 146 \text{ days}$
$= \frac{2}{5} \text{ years.}$
$\therefore S.I. = ₹ \left(15000 \times 18 \times \frac{2}{5} \times \frac{1}{100}\right) = ₹ 1080.$
$\text{Hence, amount paid} = ₹ (15000 + 1080) = ₹ 16080.$

questions.question_text: A person borrows ₹ 5000 for 2 years at 4% p.a. simple interest. He immediately lends it to another person at $6\frac{1}{4}\%$ p.a. for 2 years. Find his gain in the transaction per year.
questions.solution:
$\text{Gain in 2 yrs} = \left[ \left(5000 \times \frac{25}{4} \times \frac{2}{100}\right) - \left(5000 \times 4 \times \frac{2}{100}\right) \right]$
$= ₹ (625 - 400) = ₹ 225.$
$\therefore \text{Gain in 1 year} = \frac{225}{2} = ₹ 112.50.$

questions.question_text: Ramakant invested amounts in two different schemes A and B for five years in the ratio of 5 : 4 respectively. Scheme A offers 8% simple interest and bonus equal to 20% of the amount of interest earned in 5 years on maturity. Scheme B offers 9% simple interest. If the amount invested in scheme A was ₹ 20000, what was the total amount received on maturity from both the schemes?
questions.solution:
$\text{Let the amounts invested in Schemes A and B be } ₹ 5x \text{ and } ₹ 4x \text{ respectively. Then,}$
$5x = 20000 \Rightarrow x = 4000.$
$\therefore \text{Amount invested in Scheme B} = ₹ 16000.$
$\text{Total interest received on maturity}$
$= 120\% \text{ of } \left( \frac{20000 \times 8 \times 5}{100} + \frac{16000 \times 9 \times 5}{100} \right)$
$= ₹ (120\% \text{ of } 8000 + 7200) = ₹ (9600 + 7200)$
$= ₹ 16800.$
$\therefore \text{Total amount} = ₹ (20000 + 16000 + 16800) = ₹ 52800.$

questions.question_text: ₹ 1000 is invested at 5% per annum simple interest. If the interest is added to the principal after every 10 years, the amount will become ₹ 2000 after
questions.solution:
$\text{Amount after 10 years} = \left[ 1000 + \frac{1000 \times 5 \times 10}{100} \right] = ₹ 1500.$
$\text{Now, S.I.} = ₹ (2000 - 1500) = ₹ 500, P = ₹ 1500, R = 5\%.$
$\therefore \text{Time} = \left( \frac{500 \times 100}{1500 \times 5} \right) \text{ yrs} = 6\frac{2}{3} \text{ yrs.}$
$\text{Hence, required time} = \left( 10 + 6\frac{2}{3} \right) \text{ yrs} = 16\frac{2}{3} \text{ yrs.}$

questions.question_text: How much time will it take for an amount of ₹ 450 to yield ₹ 81 as interest at 4.5% per annum of simple interest ?
questions.solution:
$P = ₹ 450, S.I. = ₹ 81, R = 4.5\%.$
$\text{Time} = \left( \frac{100 \times 81}{450 \times 4.5} \right) \text{ years} = 4 \text{ years.}$

questions.question_text: Asmita invests an amount of ₹ 9534 @ 4 p.c.p.a. to obtain a total amount of ₹ 11442 on simple interest after a certain period. For how many years did she invest the amount to obtain the total sum?
questions.solution:
$P = ₹ 9534, S.I. = ₹ (11442 - 9534) = ₹ 1908, R = 4\%.$
$\therefore \text{Time} = \left( \frac{100 \times 1908}{9534 \times 4} \right) \text{ yrs} \approx 5 \text{ years.}$

questions.question_text: Deepak invested an amount of ₹ 21250 for 6 years. At what rate of simple interest will be obtain the total amount of ₹ 26350 at the end of 6 years?
questions.solution:
$P = ₹ 21250, S.I. = ₹ (26350 - 21250) = ₹ 5100, T = 6 \text{ years.}$
$\therefore \text{Rate} = \left( \frac{100 \times 5100}{21250 \times 6} \right)\% = 4\%.$

questions.question_text: A sum of ₹ 1600 gives a simple interest of ₹ 252 in 2 years and 4 months. The rate of interest per annum is
questions.solution:
$\text{Time} = 2 \text{ years } 4 \text{ months} = 2\frac{4}{12} \text{ years} = \frac{7}{3} \text{ years,}$
$S.I. = ₹ 252, P = ₹ 1600.$
$\text{Rate} = \left( \frac{100 \times 252 \times 3}{1600 \times 7} \right)\% = 6\frac{3}{4}\%.$

questions.question_text: At what rate of simple interest per annum can an amount of ₹ 1553.40 be obtained on the principal amount of ₹ 8630 after 3 years?
questions.solution:
$P = ₹ 8630, S.I. = ₹ 1553.40, T = 3 \text{ yrs.}$
$\therefore \text{Rate} = \left( \frac{100 \times 1553.40}{8630 \times 3} \right)\% = 6\%.$

questions.question_text: If simple interest on ₹ 600 for 4 years and on ₹ 600 for 2 years combined together is ₹ 180, find the rate of interest.
questions.solution:
$\text{Let the rate be } R\% \text{ p.a.}$
$\text{Then, } \left( \frac{600 \times R \times 4}{100} \right) + \left( \frac{600 \times R \times 2}{100} \right) = 180$
$\Rightarrow 2400R + 1200R = 18000 \Rightarrow 3600R = 18000$
$\Rightarrow R = 5\%.$

questions.question_text: Veena obtained an amount of ₹ 8376 as simple interest on a certain amount at 8 p.c.p.a. after 6 years. What is the amount invested by Veena?
questions.solution:
$S.I. = ₹ 8376, R = 8\%, T = 6 \text{ yrs.}$
$\therefore \text{Sum} = ₹ \left( \frac{100 \times 8376}{8 \times 6} \right) = ₹ 17450.$

questions.question_text: At which sum the simple interest at the rate of $3\frac{3}{4}\%$ per annum will be ₹ 210 in $1\frac{2}{3}$ years?
questions.solution:
$S.I. = ₹ 210, R = 3\frac{3}{4}\% = \frac{15}{4}\%, T = 2\frac{1}{3} \text{ yrs} = \frac{7}{3} \text{ yrs.}$
$\therefore \text{Sum} = ₹ \left( \frac{100 \times 210 \times 4 \times 3}{15 \times 7} \right) = ₹ 2400.$

questions.question_text: What is the present worth of ₹ 132 due in 2 years at 5% simple interest per annum?
questions.solution:
$\text{Let the present worth be } ₹ x. \text{ Then, } S.I. = ₹ (132 - x).$
$\therefore \left( \frac{x \times 5 \times 2}{100} \right) = 132 - x \Leftrightarrow 10x = 13200 - 100x$
$\Leftrightarrow 110x = 13200 \Leftrightarrow x = 120.$

questions.question_text: A sum fetched a total simple interest of ₹ 4016.25 at the rate of 9 p.c.p.a. in 5 years. What is the sum?
questions.solution: $\text{Principal} = ₹ \left( \frac{100 \times 4016.25}{9 \times 5} \right) = ₹ \left( \frac{401625}{45} \right) = ₹ 8925.$

questions.question_text: The simple interest at x% for x years will be ₹ x on a sum of :
questions.solution: Sum = $ rac{100	imes S.I.}{R	imes T} =  rac{100	imes x}{x	imes x} = ₹  rac{100}{x}$

questions.question_text: In 4 years, ₹ 6000 amounts to ₹ 8000. In what time at the same rate will ₹ 525 amount to ₹ 700?
₹ 6200 amounts to ₹ 9176 in 4 years at simple interest. If the interest rate is increased by 3% it would amount to how much?
Simple interest on ₹ 500 for 4 years at 6.25% per annum is equal to the simple interest on ₹ 400 at 5% per annum for a certain period of time. The period of time is
questions.solution:
Let the required time be T years.
Then, $ rac{500	imes 6.25	imes 4}{100} =  rac{400	imes 5	imes T}{100}$
$\Rightarrow T =  rac{500	imes 6.25	imes 4}{400	imes 5} = 6 rac{1}{4}$ years.

questions.question_text: A certain amount earns simple interest of ₹ 1750 after 7 years. Had the interest been 2% more, how much more interest would it have earned ?
questions.solution: We need to known the S.I; principal and time to find the rate. Since the principal is not given, so data is inadequate.

questions.question_text: ₹ 6000 becomes ₹ 7200 in 4 years at a certain rate of simple interest. If the rate becomes 1.5 times of itself, the amount of the same principal in 5 years will be
In how many years, ₹ 150 will produce the same interest @ 8% as ₹ 800 produce in 3 years @ $14\frac{1}{2}\%$ ?

The simple interest accrued on a certain principal in 5 years at the rate of 12 p.c.p.a. is ₹ 1536. What amount of simple interest would one get if one invests ₹ 1000 more than the previous principal for 2 years and at the same rate p.c.p.a.?
If ₹ 64 amounts to ₹ 83.20 in 2 years, what will ₹ 86 amount to in 4 years at the same rate percent per annum ?
The simple interest on a certain sum of money at the rate of 5% p.a. for 8 years is ₹ 840. At what rate of interest the same amount of interest can be received on the same sum after 5 years ?
questions.solution:
S.I. = ₹ 840, R = 5%, T = 8 years.
Principal = ₹ $ rac{100	imes 840}{5	imes 8} = 2100$.
Now, P = ₹ 2100, S.I. = ₹ 840, T = 5 years.
Rate = $ rac{100	imes 840}{2100	imes 5} = 8\%$.

questions.question_text: The interest on a certain deposit at 4.5% p.a. is ₹ 202.50 in one year. How much will the additional interest in one year be on the same deposit at 5% p.a. ?
A sum invested at 5% simple interest per annum grows to ₹ 504 in 4 years. The same amount at 10% simple interest per annum in $2\frac{1}{2}$ years will grow to :
questions.solution:
Let the sum be ₹ x. Then, S.I. = ₹ (504 - x).
$ rac{x	imes 5	imes 4}{100} = 504 - x \Leftrightarrow 20x = 50400 - 100x$
$\Leftrightarrow 120x = 50400 \Leftrightarrow x = 420$.
Now, P = ₹ 420, R = 10%, T = 5/2 years.
S.I. = ₹ $ rac{420	imes 10	imes 5}{100	imes 2} = 105$.
Amount = ₹ (420 + 105) = ₹ 525.

questions.question_text: If x, y, z are three sums of money such that y is the simple interest on x, z is the simple interest on y for the same time and at the same rate of interest, then we have
questions.solution:
Let time be T years and rate be R% p.a.
Then, y is the S.I. on x $\Rightarrow  rac{xRT}{100} = y$ ... (i)
And, z is the S.I. on y $\Rightarrow  rac{yRT}{100} = z \Rightarrow y =  rac{100z}{RT}$ ... (ii)
From (i) and (ii) we have: $ rac{xRT}{100} =  rac{100z}{RT}$
$\Rightarrow R^2T^2 =  rac{100^2z}{x} \Rightarrow y^2 = xz$.

questions.question_text: What will be the ratio of simple interest earned by certain amount at the same rate of interest for 6 years and that for 9 years ?
questions.solution:
Let the principal be P and rate of interest be R%
Required ratio = $ rac{ rac{P	imes R	imes 6}{100}}{ rac{P	imes R	imes 9}{100}} =  rac{6}{9} = 2:3$.

questions.question_text: Arun borrowed a sum of money from Jayant at the rate of 8% per annum simple interest for the first four years, 10% per annum for the next 6 years and 12% per annum for the period beyond 10 years. If he pays a total of ₹ 12160 as interest only at the end of 15 years, how much money did be borrow?
questions.solution:
Let the sum be ₹ x. Then,
$ rac{x	imes 8	imes 4}{100} +  rac{x	imes 10	imes 6}{100} +  rac{x	imes 12	imes 5}{100} = 12160$
$\Rightarrow 32x + 60x + 60x = 1216000$
$\Rightarrow 152x = 1216000 \Rightarrow x = 8000$.

questions.question_text: Kruti took a loan at simple interest rate of 6 p.c.p.a. in the first year and it increased by 1.5 p.c.p.a. every year. If she pays ₹ 8190 as interest at the end of 3 years, what was her loan amount?
questions.solution:
Let the loan amount be ₹ x. Then,
$ rac{6x}{100} +  rac{7.5x}{100} +  rac{9x}{100} = 8190 \Rightarrow 22.5x = 819000 \Rightarrow x = 36400$.

questions.question_text: A person deposited ₹ 400 for 2 years, ₹ 550 for 4 years and ₹ 1200 for 6 years. He received the total simple interest of ₹ 1020. The rate of interest per annum is
questions.solution:
Let the rate of interest be R% p.a.
Then, $ rac{400	imes R	imes 2}{100} +  rac{550	imes R	imes 4}{100} +  rac{1200	imes R	imes 6}{100} = 1020$
$\Rightarrow 800R + 2200R + 7200R = 102000$
$\Rightarrow 10200R = 102000 \Rightarrow R = 10$.

questions.question_text: The simple interest on a sum of money will be ₹ 600 after 10 years. If the principal is trebled after 5 years, what will be the total interest at the end of the tenth year ?
questions.solution:
Let the sum be ₹ x. Now, S.I. = ₹ 600, T = 10 years.
Rate = $ rac{100	imes 600}{x	imes 10} =  rac{6000}{x}\%$.
S.I. for first 5 years = ₹ $ rac{x	imes 6000	imes 5}{x	imes 100} = 300$.
S.I. for last 5 years = ₹ $ rac{x	imes 6000	imes 5}{x	imes 100} = 900$.
Total interest = ₹ 1200.

questions.question_text: The simple interest on ₹ 10 for 4 months at the rate of 3 paise per rupee per month is

An automobile financier claims to be lending money at simple interest, but he includes the interest every six months for calculating the principal. If he is charging an interest of 10%, the effective rate of interest becomes
questions.solution:
Let the sum be ₹ 100. Then,
S.I. for first 6 months = ₹ $ rac{100	imes 10	imes 1}{100	imes 2} = 5$.
S.I. for last 6 months = ₹ $ rac{105	imes 10	imes 1}{100	imes 2} = 5.25$.
So, amount at the end of 1 year = ₹ (100 + 5 + 5.25) = ₹ 110.25.
Effective rate = (110.25 - 100) = 10.25%.

questions.question_text: A sum of money at simple interest amounts to ₹ 815 in 3 years and to ₹ 854 in 4 years. The sum is
questions.solution:
S.I. for 1 year = ₹ $(854 - 815) = ₹ 39$.
S.I. for 3 years = ₹ $(39 	imes 3) = ₹ 117$.
$\therefore$ Principal = ₹ $(815 - 117) = ₹ 698$.

questions.question_text: A sum of money lent out at simple interest amounts to ₹ 720 after 2 years and to ₹ 1020 after a further period of 5 years. The sum is
questions.solution:
S.I. for 5 years = ₹ $(1020 - 720) = ₹ 300$.
S.I. for 2 years = $\left(\frac{300}{5} 	imes 2\right) = ₹ 120$.
$\therefore$ Principal = ₹ $(720 - 120) = ₹ 600$.

questions.question_text: A sum of money amounts to ₹ 5200 in 5 years and to ₹ 5680 in 7 years at simple interest. The rate of interest per annum is :
questions.solution:
S.I. for 2 years = ₹ $(5680 - 5200) = ₹ 480$.
S.I. for 5 years = $\left(\frac{480}{2} 	imes 5\right) = ₹ 1200$.
$\therefore$ Principal = ₹ $(5200 - 1200) = ₹ 4000$.
Hence, rate = $\left(\frac{100 	imes 1200}{4000 	imes 5}\right)\% = 6\%$.

questions.question_text: A sum of money becomes ₹ 20925 in 2 years and ₹ 24412.50 in 5 years. Find the rate of interest and the sum of money.
questions.solution:
S.I. for 3 years = ₹ $(24412.50 - 20925) = ₹ 3487.50$.
S.I. for 2 years = $\left(\frac{3487.50}{3} 	imes 2\right) = ₹ 2325$.
$\therefore$ Principal = ₹ $(20925 - 2325) = ₹ 18600$.
Hence, rate = $\left(\frac{100 	imes 2325}{18600 	imes 2}\right)\% = 6.25\%$.

questions.question_text: A certain sum of money at simple interest amounts to ₹ 1012 in $2\frac{1}{2}$ years and to ₹ 1067.20 in 4 years.
questions.solution:
S.I. for $1\frac{1}{2}$ years = ₹ $(1067.20 - 1012) = ₹ 55.20$.
S.I. for $2\frac{1}{2}$ years = $\left(55.20 	imes \frac{2}{3} \times \frac{5}{2}\right) = ₹ 92$.
$\therefore$ Principal = ₹ $(1012 - 92) = ₹ 920$.
Hence, rate = $\left(\frac{100 	imes 92 	imes 2}{920 	imes 5}\right)\% = 4\%$.

questions.question_text: In how many years will a sum of money double itself at 18.75% per annum simple interest?
questions.solution:
Let sum = ₹ $x$. Then, S.I. = ₹ $x$.
$\therefore$ Time = $\left(\frac{100 	imes S.I.}{P 	imes R}\right) = \left(\frac{100 	imes x}{x 	imes 18.75}\right)$ years = $\frac{26}{3}$ years
$= 5\frac{1}{3}$ years = 5 years 4 months.

questions.question_text: At what rate percent of simple interest will a sum of money double itself in 12 years ?
questions.solution:
Let sum = ₹ $x$. Then, S.I. = ₹ $x$.
$\therefore$ Rate = $\left(\frac{100 	imes S.I.}{P 	imes T}\right) = \left(\frac{100 	imes x}{x 	imes 12}\right)\% = \frac{25}{3}\% = 8\frac{1}{3}\%$.

questions.question_text: The rate at which a sum becomes four times of itself in 15 years at S.I., will be
questions.solution:
Let sum = ₹ $x$. Then, S.I. = ₹ $3x$.
$\therefore$ Rate = $\left(\frac{100 	imes S.I.}{P 	imes T}\right) = \left(\frac{100 	imes 3x}{x 	imes 15}\right)\% = 20\%$.

questions.question_text: A sum of money at a simple rate of interest $i_1$, doubles in 5 years. At another simple rate of interest $i_2$, it becomes three times in 12 years. Then, the two rates of interest $i_1$ and $i_2$ respectively are
questions.solution:
Case I. Let sum = ₹ $x$. Then, S.I. = ₹ $x$.
$\therefore$ Rate = $\left(\frac{100 	imes S.I.}{P 	imes T}\right) = \left(\frac{100 	imes x}{x 	imes 5}\right)\% = 20\%$.
Case II. Let sum = ₹ $x$. Then, S.I. = ₹ $2x$.
$\therefore$ Rate = $\left(\frac{100 	imes S.I.}{P 	imes T}\right) = \left(\frac{100 	imes 2x}{x 	imes 12}\right)\% = \frac{50}{3}\% = 16\frac{2}{3}\%$.

questions.question_text: A sum of money at simple interest doubles in 7 years. It will become four times in:
questions.solution:
Let sum = ₹ $x$. Then, S.I. = ₹ $x$.
$\therefore$ Rate = $\left(\frac{100 	imes x}{x 	imes 7}\right)\% = \frac{100}{7}\%$.
Now, sum = ₹ $x$, S.I. = ₹ $3x$, Rate = $\frac{100}{7}\%$.
$\therefore$ Time = $\left(\frac{100 	imes 3x}{x 	imes \frac{100}{7}}\right)$ years = 21 years.

questions.question_text: A sum of money trebles itself in 15 years 6 months. In how many years would it double itself ?
questions.solution:
Let sum = ₹ $x$.
Then, S.I. = ₹ $2x$, Time = $15\frac{1}{2}$ years = $\frac{31}{2}$ years.
$\therefore$ Rate = $\left(\frac{100 	imes 2x}{x 	imes \frac{31}{2}}\right)\% = \frac{400}{31}\%$.
Now, sum = ₹ $x$, S.I. = ₹ $x$, Rate = $\frac{400}{31}\%$.
$\therefore$ Time = $\left(\frac{100 	imes x}{x 	imes \frac{400}{31}}\right)$ years = $\frac{31}{4}$ years = 7 years 9 months.

questions.question_text: If a sum doubles in 6 years, how much will it be in 8 years?
questions.solution:
Let sum = ₹ $x$. Then, S.I. = ₹ $x$, Time = 16 years.
$\therefore$ Rate = $\left(\frac{100 	imes x}{x 	imes 16}\right)\% = \frac{25}{4}\% = 6\frac{1}{4}\%$.
Now, sum = ₹ $x$, Time = 8 years, Rate = $6\frac{1}{4}\%$.
$\therefore$ S.I. = $\left(\frac{x 	imes 25 	imes 8}{100 	imes 4}\right) = \frac{x}{2}$.
So, amount = $\left(x + \frac{x}{2}\right) = \frac{3x}{2} = 1\frac{1}{2}$ times.

questions.passage_text:
Consider the following statements
If a sum of money is lent at simple interest, then the
1. money gets doubled in 5 years if the rate of interest is $16 rac{2}{3}\%$.
2. money gets doubled in 5 years if the rate of interest is 20%.
3. money becomes four times in 10 years if it gets doubled in 5 years.
questions.question_text: Of these statements,
questions.solution:
Let sum be $x$. Then, S.I. = $x$.
1. Time = $\left(\frac{100 	imes x}{x 	imes \frac{50}{3}}\right)$ years = 6 years (False)
2. Time = $\left(\frac{100 	imes x}{x 	imes 20}\right)$ years = 5 years (True)
3. Suppose sum = $x$. Then, S.I. = $x$ and Time = 5 years.
Rate = $\left(\frac{100 	imes x}{x 	imes 5}\right)\% = 20\%$.
Now, sum = $x$, S.I. = $3x$ and Rate = 20%.
$\therefore$ Time = $\left(\frac{100 	imes 3x}{x 	imes 20}\right)$ years = 15 years (False)
So, 2 alone is correct.

questions.question_text: The simple interest on a sum of money at 8% per annum for 6 years is half the sum. The sum is :
questions.solution:
Let sum = $x$.
Then, S.I. = $\frac{x}{2}$.
$\therefore \frac{x}{2} = \frac{x 	imes 8 	imes 6}{100}$. Clearly, data is inadequate.

questions.question_text: At what rate percent per annum will the simple interest on a sum of money be $ rac{2}{5}$ of the amount in 10 years ?
questions.solution:
Let sum = $x$. Then, S.I. = $\frac{2x}{5}$, Time = 10 years.
$\therefore$ Rate = $\left(\frac{100 	imes 2x}{5 	imes x 	imes 10}\right)\% = 4\%$.

questions.question_text: In how much time would the simple interest on a certain sum be 0.125 times the principal at 10% per annum ?
questions.solution:
Let sum = $x$. Then, S.I. = $0.125x = \frac{1}{8}x$, R = 10%.
$\therefore$ Rate = $\left(\frac{100 	imes x}{8 	imes x 	imes 10}\right)$ years = $\frac{5}{4}$ years = $1\frac{1}{4}$ years.

questions.question_text: How long will it take a sum of money invested at 5% p.a. S.I. to increase its value by 40%?
questions.solution:
Let the sum be $x$.
Then, S.I. = 40% of $x = \frac{2}{5}x$; Rate = 5%.
$\therefore$ Time = $\left(\frac{2 	imes 100}{5 	imes 5}\right)$ years = 8 years.

questions.question_text: A sum of money becomes $ rac{7}{6}$ of itself in 3 years at a certain rate of simple interest. The rate per annum is
questions.solution:
Let sum = $x$. Then, amount = $\frac{7x}{6}$.
S.I. = $\left(\frac{7x}{6} - x\right) = \frac{x}{6}$; Time = 3 years.
$\therefore$ Rate = $\left(\frac{100 	imes x}{6 	imes x 	imes 3}\right)\% = \frac{50}{9}\% = 5\frac{5}{9}\%$.

questions.question_text: If the simple interest for 6 years be equal to 30% of the principal, it will be equal to the principal after
questions.solution:
Let sum = ₹ $x$. Then, S.I. = 30% of ₹ $x = \frac{3x}{10}$.
Time = 6 years.
$\therefore$ Rate = $\left(\frac{100 	imes 3x}{10 	imes x 	imes 6}\right)\% = 5\%$.
Now, sum = ₹ $x$, S.I. = ₹ $x$, Rate = 5%.
$\therefore$ Time = $\left(\frac{100 	imes x}{5 	imes x}\right)$ years = 20 years.

questions.question_text: Simple interest on a certain sum at a certain annual rate of interest is $ rac{1}{9}$ of the sum. If the numbers representing rate percent and time in years be equal, then the rate of interest is
questions.solution:
Let sum = $x$. Then, S.I. = $\frac{x}{9}$.
Let rate = R% and time = R years.
$\therefore \left(\frac{x 	imes R 	imes R}{100}\right) = \frac{x}{9} \Leftrightarrow R^2 = \frac{100}{9} \Leftrightarrow R = \frac{10}{3} = 3\frac{1}{3}$.
Hence, rate = $3\frac{1}{3}\%$.

questions.question_text: Simple interest on a certain amount is $ rac{9}{16}$ of the principal. If the numbers representing the rate of interest in percent and time in years be equal, then time, for which the principal is lent out, is
questions.solution:
Let sum = x. Then, S.I. = $ rac{9}{16}x$.
Let rate = R% and time = R years.
$	herefore  rac{x	imes R	imes R}{100} =  rac{9x}{16}$
$	herefore R^2 =  rac{900}{16}$
$R =  rac{30}{4} = 7 rac{1}{2}$.
Hence, time = $7 rac{1}{2}$ years.

questions.question_text: A lends ₹ 2500 to B and a certain sum to C at the same time at 7% p.a. simple interest. If after 4 years, A altogether receives ₹ 1120 as interest from B and C, then the sum lent to C is :
questions.solution:
Let the sum lent to C be ₹ x.
Then, $ rac{2500	imes7	imes4}{100} +  rac{x	imes7	imes4}{100} = 1120$
$ rac{7}{25}x = (1120 - 700)$
$x =  rac{420	imes25}{7} = 1500$.

questions.question_text: Two equal sums of money were lent at simple interest at 11% p.a. for $1 rac{3}{2}$ years and $1 rac{4}{2}$ years respectively. If the difference in interests for two periods was ₹ 412.50, then each sum is:
questions.solution:
Let each sum be ₹ x.
Then, $ rac{x	imes11	imes9}{100	imes2} -  rac{x	imes11	imes7}{100	imes2} = 412.50$
$(99x - 77x) = 82500$
$22x = 82500$
$x = 3750$.

questions.question_text: If the simple interest on a certain sum for 15 months at $1 rac{7}{2}\%$ per annum exceeds the simple interest on the same sum for 8 months at $1 rac{12}{2}\%$ per annum by ₹ 32.50, then the sum (in ₹) is :
questions.solution:
Let the sum be ₹ x.
Then, $ rac{x	imes15	imes5}{2	imes4	imes100} -  rac{x	imes25	imes1}{2	imes3	imes100} = 32.50$
$ rac{75x}{8} -  rac{25x}{3} = 3250$
$25x = 3250	imes24$
$x =  rac{3250	imes24}{25} = 3120$.

questions.question_text: Sujata invested ₹ 7500 at simple interest @ 11 p.c.p.a. She further invested some amount at simple interest @ 15 p.c.p.a. Total interest earned at the end of the year became 12 p.c.p.a. Find the amount invested at the rate of 15 p.c.p.a.

A man invests a certain sum of money at 6% p.a. simple interest and another sum at 7% p.a. simple interest. His income from interest after 2 years was ₹ 354. One-fourth of the first sum is equal to one-fifth of the second sum. The total sum invested was :
questions.solution:
Let the sums be x and y.
$ rac{x	imes6	imes2}{100} +  rac{y	imes7	imes2}{100} = 354$ or $6x + 7y = 17700$ ... (i)
Also, $ rac{x}{4} =  rac{y}{5}$ or $5x - 4y = 0$ ... (ii)
Solving (i) and (ii), we get : x = 1200 and y = 1500.
$	herefore$ Total sum = ₹ 2700.

questions.question_text: Rahul borrowed a sum of ₹ 1150 from Amit at the simple interest rate of 6 p.c.p.a. for 3 years. He then added some more money to the borrowed sum and lent it to Sachin for the same time at 9 p.c.p.a. simple interest. If Rahul gains ₹ 274.95 by way of interest on borrowed sum as well as his own amount from the whole transaction, then what is the sum lent by him to Sachin?
questions.solution:
Let the money added by Rahul be ₹ x.
Then, $ rac{(1150 + x)	imes9	imes3}{100} -  rac{1150	imes6	imes3}{100} = 274.95$
$1150	imes27 + 27x - 1150	imes18 = 27495$
$27x + 1150	imes(27 - 18) = 27495$
$27x = 27495 - 10350 = 17145$
$x = 635$.
So, sum lent by Rahul to Sachin = ₹ (1150 + 635) = ₹ 1785.

questions.question_text: A person invested some amount at the rate of 12% simple interest and a certain amount at the rate of 10% simple interest. He received yearly interest of ₹ 130. But if he had interchanged the amounts invested, he would have received ₹ 4 more as interest. How much did he invest at 12% simple interest?
questions.solution:
Let the amount invested at 12% be ₹ x and that invested at 10% be ₹ y.
Then, 12% of x + 10% of y = 130
$12x + 10y = 13000$ or $6x + 5y = 6500$ ... (i)
And, 10% of x + 12% of y = 134
$10x + 12y = 13400$ or $5x + 6y = 6700$ ... (ii)
Adding (i) and (ii), we get: $11(x + y) = 13200$
$x + y = 1200$ ... (iii)
Subtracting (i) from (ii), we get: $-x + y = 200$ ... (iv)
Adding (iii) and (iv), we get: $2y = 1400$ or $y = 700$.
Hence, amount invested at 12% = (1200 - 700) = ₹ 500.

questions.question_text: A person borrowed ₹ 500 @ 3% per annum S.I. and ₹ 600 @ $1 rac{4}{2}\%$ per annum on the agreement that the whole sum will be returned only when the total interest becomes ₹ 126. The number of years, after which the borrowed sum is to be returned, is :
questions.solution:
Let the time be x years.
Then, $ rac{500	imes3	imes x}{100} +  rac{600	imes9	imes x}{100	imes2} = 126$
$15x + 27x = 126$
$42x = 126$
$x = 3$.
$	herefore$ Required time = 3 years.

questions.question_text: Two equal sums of money are lent at the same time at 8% and 7% per annum simple interest. The former is recovered 6 months earlier than the latter and the amount in each case is ₹ 2560. The sum and the time for which the sums of money are lent out are
questions.solution:
Let each sum = ₹ x.
Let the first sum be invested for $(T -  rac{1}{2})$ years and the second sum for T years.
Then, $x +  rac{x	imes8	imes(T -  rac{1}{2})}{100} = 2560$
$100x + 8xT - 4x = 256000$
$96x + 8xT = 256000$ ... (i)
And, $x +  rac{x	imes7	imes T}{100} = 2560$
$100x + 7xT = 256000$ ... (ii)
From (i) and (ii), we get: $96x + 8xT = 100x + 7xT$
$4x = xT$
$T = 4$.
Putting T = 4 in (i), we get: $96x + 32x = 256000$
$128x = 256000$
$x = 2000$.

questions.question_text: A lent ₹ 5000 to B for 2 years and ₹ 3000 to C for 4 years on simple interest at the same rate of interest and received ₹ 2200 in all from both of them as interest. The rate of interest per annum is
questions.solution:
Let the rate be R% p.a. Then,
$ rac{5000	imes R	imes 2}{100} +  rac{3000	imes R	imes 4}{100} = 2200$
$100R + 120R = 2200$
$R =  rac{2200}{220} = 10$.
$	herefore$ Rate = 10%.

questions.question_text: A sum of ₹ 725 is lent in the beginning of a year at a certain rate of interest. After 8 months, a sum of ₹ 362.50 more is lent but at the rate twice the former. At the end of the year, ₹ 33.50 is earned as interest from both the loans. What was the original rate of interest ?
questions.solution:
Let the original rate be R%. Then, new rate = (2R)%.
$ rac{725	imes R	imes 1}{100} +  rac{362.50	imes 2R	imes 1}{100	imes 3} = 33.50$
$(2175 + 725)R = 33.50	imes 100	imes 3 = 10050$
$R =  rac{10050}{2900} = 3.46$.
$	herefore$ Original rate = 3.46%.

questions.question_text: The difference between the simple interest received from two different sources on ₹ 1500 for 3 years is ₹ 13.50. The difference between their rates of interest is

Peter invested an amount of ₹ 12,000 at the rate of 10 p.c.p.a. simple interest and another amount at the rate of 20 p.c.p.a. simple interest. The total interest earned at the end of one year on the total amount invested became 14 p.c.p.a. Find the total amount invested.
questions.solution:
Let the second amount be ₹ x. Then,
$ rac{12000	imes 10	imes 1}{100} +  rac{x	imes 20	imes 1}{100} =  rac{(12000 + x)	imes 14	imes 1}{100}$
$12000 + 20x = 168000 + 14x$
$6x = 48000$
$x = 8000$.
$	herefore$ Total investment = ₹ (12000 + 8000) = ₹ 20000.

questions.question_text: A man invested ₹ 5000 at some rate of simple interest and ₹ 4000 at 1 percent higher rate of interest. If the interest in both the cases after 4 years is same, the rate of interest in the former case is
questions.solution:
Let the rates of interest in the former and latter cases be R% and (R + 1)% p.a.
Then, $5000	imes R	imes 4 = 4000	imes (R + 1)	imes 4$
$ rac{5000	imes R	imes 4}{4000	imes 4} = R + 1$
$1.25R = R + 1$
$0.25R = 1$
$R = 4$.
Hence, required rate = 4% p.a.

questions.question_text: What should be the least number of years in which the simple interest on ₹ 2600 at $2 rac{6}{3}\%$ will be an exact number of rupees ?
questions.solution: S.I. = $ rac{2600	imes 20	imes T}{3	imes 100} =  rac{520}{3}	imes T$, which is an exact number of rupees when T = 3 years.

questions.question_text: The rates of simple interest in two banks A and B are in the ratio 5 : 4. A person wants to deposit his total savings in two banks in such a way that he received equal half-yearly interest from both. He should deposit the savings in banks A and B in the ratio:
questions.solution:
Let the savings be X and Y and the rates of simple interest be 5x and 4x respectively.
Then, $ rac{X	imes 5x	imes 1}{2	imes 100} =  rac{Y	imes 4x	imes 1}{2	imes 100}$
$ rac{X}{Y} =  rac{4}{5}$ i.e., X : Y = 4 : 5.

questions.question_text: A sum was put at simple interest at a certain rate for 3 years. Had it been put at 1% higher rate, it would have fetched ₹ 5100 more. The sum is
questions.solution:
Let the sum be ₹ x and original rate be R%.
Then, $ rac{x	imes (R + 1)	imes 3}{100} -  rac{x	imes R	imes 3}{100} = 5100$
$3Rx + 3x - 3Rx = 510000$
$3x = 510000$
$x = 170000$.
Hence, sum = ₹ 170000.

questions.question_text: If the annual rate of simple interest increases from 10% to $1 rac{12}{2}\%$, a man’s yearly income increases by ₹ 1250. His principal (in ₹) is
questions.solution:
Let the sum be ₹ x.
Then, $ rac{x	imes 25	imes 1}{2	imes 100} -  rac{x	imes 10	imes 1}{100} = 1250$
$25x - 20x = 250000$
$5x = 250000$
$x = 50000$.

questions.question_text: A moneylender finds that due to a fall in the annual rate of interest from 8% to $3 rac{7}{4}\%$, his yearly income diminishes by ₹ 61.50. His capital is
questions.solution:
Let the capital be ₹ x.
Then, $ rac{x	imes 8	imes 1}{100} -  rac{x	imes 31	imes 1}{4	imes 100} = 61.50$
$32x - 31x = 6150	imes 4$
$x = 24600$.

questions.question_text: What equal instalment of annual payment will discharge a debt which is due as ₹ 848 at the end of 4 years at 4% per annum simple interest ?
questions.solution:
Let the annual instalment be ₹ $x$.
Then,
$
\left[x+\left(\frac{x\times3\times4}{100}\right)\right]+\left[x+\left(\frac{x\times2\times4}{100}\right)\right]
+\left[x+\left(\frac{x\times1\times4}{100}\right)\right]+x=848
$
$
\left(\frac{28}{25}\right)x+\left(\frac{27}{25}\right)x+\left(\frac{26}{25}\right)x+x=848
$
$106x=848\times25=21200$
$x=200$

questions.question_text: A sum of ₹ 10 is lent to be returned in 11 monthly instalments of ₹ 1 each, interest being simple. The rate of interest is :
questions.solution:
₹ $10$ + S.I. on ₹ $10$ for $11$ months
= ₹ $11$ + S.I. on ₹ $1$ for $(1+2+3+4+...+10)$ months
⇒ ₹ $10$ + S.I. on ₹ $1$ for $110$ months
= ₹ $11$ + S.I. on ₹ $1$ for $55$ months
⇒ S.I. on ₹ $1$ for $55$ months = ₹ $1$.
∴ Rate = $\left(\frac{100\times12}{1\times55}\right)\%=21\frac{9}{11}\%$.

questions.question_text: A person takes a loan of ₹ 200 at 5% simple interest. He returns ₹ 100 at the end of 1 year. In order to clear his dues at the end of 2 years, he would pay :
questions.solution:
Amount to be paid = $\left(100+\frac{200\times5\times1}{100}+\frac{100\times5\times1}{100}\right)$
= ₹ $115$.

questions.question_text: The price of a T.V. set worth ₹ 20,000 is to be paid in 20 instalments of ₹ 1000 each. If the rate of interest be 6% per annum, and the first instalment be paid at the time of purchase, then the value of the last instalment covering the interest as well will be :
questions.solution:
Money paid in cash = ₹ $1000$.
Balance payment = ₹ $(20000-1000)=₹19000$.

questions.question_text: A computer is available for ₹ 39000 cash or ₹ 17000 as cash down payment followed by five monthly instalments of ₹ 4800 each. What is the rate of interest under the instalment plan?
questions.solution:
Total cost of the computer = ₹ $39000$.
Down payment = ₹ $17000$
Balance = ₹ $(39000-17000)=₹22000$.
Let the rate of interest be $R\%$ p.a.
Amount of ₹ $22000$ for $5$ months
= $\left(22000+22000\times\frac{5}{12}\times\frac{R}{100}\right)$
= $\left(22000+\frac{275R}{3}\right)$

questions.question_text: If the rate increases by 2%, the simple interest received on a sum of money increases by ₹ 108. If the time period is increased by 2 years, the simple interest on the same sum increases by ₹ 180. The sum is:
questions.solution:
Let the sum be ₹ $x$, rate be $R\%$ p.a. and time be $T$ years.
Then, $\left[\frac{x\times R\times(T+2)}{100}-\frac{x\times R\times T}{100}\right]=108$
⇔ $2xT=10800$ ... (i)
And, $\left[\frac{x\times R\times(T+2)}{100}-\frac{x\times R\times T}{100}\right]=180$
⇔ $2xR=18000$ ... (ii)
Clearly, from (i) and (ii), we cannot find the value of $x$.
So, the data is inadequate.

questions.question_text: Vishwas borrowed a total amount of ₹ 30000, part of it on simple interest rate of 12 p.c.p.a. and remaining on simple interest rate of 10 p.c.p.a. If at the end of 2 years he paid in all ₹ 36480 to settle the loan amount, what was the amount borrowed at 12 p.c.p.a?
questions.solution:
Let the sum borrowed at $12\%$ p.a. be ₹ $x$ and that borrowed at $10\%$ p.a. be ₹ $(30000-x)$.
S.I. at the end of $2$ years = ₹ $(36480-30000)=₹6480$.
∴ $\left[\frac{x\times12\times2}{100}+\frac{(30000-x)\times10\times2}{100}\right]=6480$
⇔ $24x+600000-20x=648000$
⇔ $4x=48000$ ⇔ $x=12000$.

questions.passage_text: A man divided his share between his sons A and B in such a way that the interest received by A at 15% p.a. for 3 years is double the interest received by B at 12% p.a. for 5 years.
questions.question_text: In what ratio was his share divided?
questions.solution:
Let A's share = ₹ $x$ and B's share = ₹ $y$.
Then, $\frac{x\times15\times3}{100}=2\times\frac{y\times12\times5}{100}$
⇒ $45x=120y$
⇒ $\frac{x}{y}=\frac{120}{45}=\frac{8}{3}$.

questions.question_text: A sum of ₹ 18750 is left by a will by a father to be divided between the two sons, 12 and 14 years of age, so that when they attain maturity at 18, the amount (principal + interest) received by each at 5 per cent simple interest will be the same. Find the sum allotted at present to each son.
questions.solution:
Let the two sums be ₹ $x$ and ₹ $(18750-x)$.
Then, $x+\frac{x\times5\times6}{100}=(18750-x)+\frac{(18750-x)\times5\times4}{100}$
⇔ $x+\frac{30x}{100}=(18750-x)+\frac{20(18750-x)}{100}$
⇔ $2x+\frac{x}{2}=22500$
⇔ $x=\left(\frac{22500\times2}{5}\right)=9000$.
Hence, the two sums are ₹ $9000$ and ₹ $9750$.

questions.question_text: A certain sum of money is invested at an interest rate of 5% per annum and a second sum, twice as large as the first, is invested at 5.5% p.a. The total amount of interest earned from the two investments together is ₹ 1000 per year and the interest is withdrawn every year. The second sum invested is
questions.solution:
Let the two sums be ₹ $x$ and ₹ $2x$.
Then, $\frac{x\times5\times2}{100}+\frac{2x\times5.5}{100}=1000$
⇒ $5x+11x=100000$
⇒ $16x=100000$ ⇔ $x=6250$.
Hence, second sum ₹ $(6250\times2)=₹12500$.

questions.question_text: I had ₹ 10000 with me. Out of this money I lent some money to A for 2 years @ 15% simple interest. I lent the remaining money to B for an equal number of years @ 18% simple interest. After 2 years, I found that A had given me ₹ 360 more as interest as compared to B. The amount of money which I had lent to B must have been
questions.solution:
Let the sum lent to A be ₹ $x$ and that lent to B be ₹ $(10000-x)$.
Then, $\frac{x\times15\times2}{100}+\frac{(10000-x)\times18\times2}{100}=360$
⇒ $30x-360000+36x=36000$
⇒ $66x=396000$
⇒ $x=6000$.
Hence, sum lent to B = ₹ $(10000-6000)=₹4000$.

questions.question_text: A sum of ₹ 2600 is lent out in two parts in such a way that the interest on one part at 10% for 5 years is equal to that on another at 9% for 6 years. The sum lent out at 10% is :
questions.solution:
Let the sum lent at $10\%$ be ₹ $x$ and that lent at $9\%$ be ₹ $(2600-x)$. Then,
$\frac{x\times10\times5}{100}+\frac{(2600-x)\times9\times6}{100}=2600$
⇔ $50x=(2600\times54)-54x$
⇒ $x=\left(\frac{2600\times54}{104}\right)=1350$.
∴ Sum lent at $10\% = ₹ 1350$.

questions.question_text: A sum of ₹ 1550 was lent partly at 5% and partly at 8% p.a. simple interest. The total interest received after 3 years was ₹ 300. The ratio of the money lent at 5% to that lent at 8% is :
questions.solution:
Let the sum lent at $5\%$ be ₹ $x$ and that lent at $8\%$ be ₹ $(1550-x)$. Then,
$\left[\frac{x\times5\times3}{100}+\frac{(1550-x)\times8\times3}{100}\right]=300$
⇔ $15x-24x+(1550\times24)=30000$
⇔ $9x=7200$ ⇔ $x=800$.
∴ Required ratio = $800:750=16:15$.

questions.question_text: A man lends ₹ 10,000 in four parts. If he gets 8% on ₹ 2000; $7 rac{1}{2}$% on ₹ 4000 and $8 rac{1}{2}$% on ₹ 1400; what percent must he get for the remainder, if his average annual interest is 8.13% ?
questions.solution:
Let the required rate be $R$. Then,
$\left(\frac{2000\times8\times1}{100}\right)+\left(\frac{4000\times15\times1}{100}\right)+\left(\frac{1400\times17\times1}{100}\right)+\left(\frac{2600\times R\times1}{100}\right)=\left(\frac{813}{10000}\times10000\right)$
⇔ $160+300+119+26R=813$ ⇔ $R=9$.

questions.question_text: An amount of ₹ 1,00,000 is invested in two types of shares. The first yields an interest of 9% p.a. and the second, 11% p.a. If the total interest at the end of one year is $9 rac{3}{4}$%, then the amount invested in each share was
questions.solution:
Let the sum invested at $9\%$ be ₹ $x$ and that invested at $11\%$ be ₹ $(100000-x)$.
Then, $\left[\frac{x\times9\times1}{100}+\frac{(100000-x)\times11\times1}{100}\right]=\left(\frac{39}{4}\times\frac{1}{100}\times100000\right)$
⇔ $\frac{9x+1100000-11x}{100}=\frac{39000}{4}=9750$
⇔ $2x=(1100000-975000)=125000$ ⇔ $x=62500$.
∴ Sum invested at $9\% = ₹ 62500$.
Sum invested at $11\% = ₹ (100000-62500)=₹37500$.

questions.question_text: David invested certain amount in three different schemes A, B and C with the rate of interest 10% p.a., 12% p.a. and 15% p.a. respectively. If the total interest accrued in one year was ₹ 3200 and the amount invested in Scheme C was 150% of the amount invested in Scheme A and 240% of the amount invested in Scheme B, what was the amount invested in Scheme B ?
questions.solution:
Let $x, y$ and $z$ be the amounts invested in schemes A, B and C respectively. Then,
$\left(\frac{x\times10\times1}{100}\right)+\left(\frac{y\times12\times1}{100}\right)+\left(\frac{z\times15\times1}{100}\right)=3200$
⇔ $10x+12y+15z=320000$ ... (i)
Now, $z=240\%$ of $y=\frac{12}{5}y$ ... (ii)
And, $z=150\%$ of $x=\frac{3}{2}x$

questions.question_text: A sum of ₹ 1440 is lent out in three parts in such a way that the interests on first part at 2% for 3 years, second part at 3% for 4 years and third part at 4% for 5 years are equal. Then the difference between the largest and the smallest sum is
questions.solution:
Let the parts be ₹ $x$, ₹ $y$ and ₹ $[1440-(x+y)]$. Then,
$\frac{x\times2\times3}{100}=\frac{y\times3\times4}{100}=\frac{[1440-(x+y)]\times4\times5}{100}$
∴ $6x=12y$ or $x=2y$.
So, $\frac{x\times2\times3}{100}+\frac{[1400-(x+y)]\times4\times5}{100}$
⇒ $12y=(1440-3y)\times20$ ⇔ $72y=28800$ ⇔ $y=400$.
First part = $x=2y=₹800$, Second part = ₹ $400$,
Third part = ₹ $[1440-(800+400)]=₹240$.
∴ Required difference = ₹ $(800-240)=₹560$.

questions.question_text: A person invests money in three different schemes for 6 years, 10 years and 12 years at 10 percent, 12 percent and 15 percent simple interest respectively. At the completion of each scheme, he gets the same interest. The ratio of his investments is
questions.solution:
Let the three amounts be ₹ $x$, ₹ $y$ and ₹ $z$.
Then, $\frac{x\times10\times6}{100}=\frac{y\times12\times10}{100}=\frac{z\times15\times12}{100}$
⇒ $60x=120y=180z$ ⇔ $x=2y=3z=k$ (say)
⇒ $x=k, y=\frac{k}{2}, z=\frac{k}{3}$
⇒ $x:y:z=k:\frac{k}{2}:\frac{k}{3}=6:3:2$.

questions.question_text: Divide ₹ 2379 into 3 parts so that their amounts after 2, 3 and 4 years respectively may be equal, the rate of interest being 5% per annum at simple interest. The first part is
questions.solution:
Let the parts be $x, y$ and $z$.
$x+\left(x\times\frac{5}{100}\times\frac{5}{100}\right)=y+\left(y\times\frac{3}{100}\times\frac{5}{100}\right)=z+\left(z\times\frac{4}{100}\times\frac{5}{100}\right)$
⇒ $\frac{11x}{10}=\frac{23y}{20}=\frac{6z}{5}=k$
⇒ $x=\frac{10k}{11}, y=\frac{20k}{23}, z=\frac{5k}{6}$.
But $x+y+z=2379$
⇒ $\frac{10k}{11}+\frac{20k}{23}+\frac{5k}{6}=2379$
⇒ $1380k+1320k+1265k=2379\times11\times23\times6$
⇒ $k=\frac{2379\times11\times23\times6}{3965}=3\times11\times23\times6$
$x=\left(\frac{10}{11}\times\frac{3\times11\times23\times6}{5}\right)=828$.
Hence, the first part is ₹ $828$.

questions.question_text: A man invested $ rac{1}{3}$ of his capital at 7%; $ rac{1}{4}$ at 8% and the remainder at 10%. If his annual income is ₹ 561, the capital is
questions.solution:
Let total capital be ₹ $x$.
Then, $\left(\frac{x}{3}\times\frac{7}{100}\times1\right)+\left(\frac{x}{4}\times\frac{8}{100}\times1\right)+\left(\frac{5x}{12}\times\frac{10}{100}\times1\right)=561$
⇔ $\frac{7x}{300}+\frac{8x}{400}+\frac{5x}{1200}=561$
⇔ $\frac{7x}{300}+\frac{x}{50}+\frac{x}{24}=561$ ⇔ $51x=(561\times600)$
⇔ $x=\left(\frac{561\times600}{51}\right)=6600$.

questions.question_text: Find the amount to be received after 2 years 6 months at the rate of 5% p.a. of simple interest on a sum of ₹ 3200.
questions.solution:
Rate of interest = 5% p.a.
Sum = ₹ 3200
Time = 2 years 6 months = $2 rac{1}{2}$ years = $ rac{5}{2}$ years
S.I. = $ rac{	ext{Principal} 	imes 	ext{Time} 	imes 	ext{Rate}}{100} =  rac{3200 	imes 5 	imes 5}{2 	imes 100}$ = ₹ 400
∴ Amount = ₹ Sum + S.I. = (3200 + 400) = ₹ 3600

questions.question_text: A man buys a TV priced at ₹ 16000. He pays ₹ 4000 at once and the rest after 15 months on which he is charged a simple interest at the rate of 12% per year. The total amount he pays for the TV is
questions.solution:
Price paid = ₹ 4000
Rest price = ₹ 12000
Rate = 12%
∴ $ rac{12000 	imes 12 	imes 5}{100 	imes 12}$ = ₹ 1800
∴ Amount paid after 15 months
= 12000 + 1800 = 13800
∴ Total amount paid for TV = 4000 + 13800 = 17800

questions.question_text: A sum becomes its double in 10 years. Find the annual rate of simple interest.
questions.solution:
Let sum be ₹ $x$ and let S.I. = ₹ $x$
Time = 10 years
∴ Rate = $ rac{	ext{S.I.} 	imes 100}{	ext{Principal} 	imes 	ext{Time}}$
= $ rac{x 	imes 100}{x 	imes 10}$ = 10% per annum.

questions.question_text: The interest earned on ₹ 4000 when invested in Scheme A for two years at 7% p.a. simple interest is half of the interest earned when ₹ ‘X’ is invested for five years in the same scheme at the same rate of interest. What is the value of X?
questions.solution:
Simple Interest earned in scheme
A = $ rac{	ext{PRT}}{100}$
= $ rac{4000 	imes 7 	imes 2}{100}$ = ₹ 560
Interest earned in scheme B = 560 $	imes$ 2
= ₹ 1120
Let ‘A’ be the principal amount invested in scheme B
Interest = $ rac{	ext{Principal} 	imes 	ext{Rate} 	imes 	ext{Time}}{100}$
1120 = $ rac{	ext{A} 	imes 7 	imes 5}{100}$
A = ₹ 3200

questions.question_text: The interest earned on ₹ 15000 in 3 years at simple interest is ₹ 5400. Find the rate of interest per annum
questions.solution:
Rate = $ rac{	ext{S.I.} 	imes 100}{	ext{Principal} 	imes 	ext{Time}} =  rac{5400 	imes 100}{15000 	imes 3}$
= 12% per annum.

questions.question_text: The sum invested in Scheme B is thrice the sum invested in Scheme A. The investment in Scheme A is made for 4 years at 8% p.a. simple interest and in Scheme B for 2 years at 13% p.a. simple interest. The total interest earned from both the schemes is ₹ 1320. How much amount was invested in Scheme A?
questions.solution:
Let the amount invested in scheme A be ₹ $x$ and
that in B be ₹ $3x$.
Then, $ rac{x 	imes 4 	imes 8}{100} +  rac{3x 	imes 2 	imes 13}{100} = 1320$
or, $ rac{32x}{100} +  rac{78x}{100} = 1320$
or, $ rac{110x}{100} = 1320$
∴ $x =  rac{1320 	imes 100}{110}$ = ₹ 1200
